_README: RECONCILED facts.json for romeforamericans — built from the Claude draft + 4-AI cross-check (ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek) + Perplexity, with load-bearing/YMYL facts web-verified against current sources. This is the ONLY facts file the writer reads. Volatile values stay serve_live. Recommendations are pooled across sources.

_meta.ai_source: reconciled (claude draft + chatgpt+gemini+grok+deepseek cross-check; load-bearing facts web-verified)

value: Italian
CET (UTC+1) / CEST (UTC+2) in summer

power_plug_voltage.value: Types C/F/L, 230V, 50Hz — US travelers need a plug adapter and, for some devices, awareness of voltage

visa._note: YMYL + change-prone. EES live since Apr 2026; ETIAS launches late 2026. verify_recommended.

fee.value: €0 now; ETIAS €7 when it launches (free under 18 / over 70)

processing_time.value: ETIAS: usually minutes, up to 96h for manual review

online_authorization_required.value: Not yet. ETIAS targeted for Q4/late 2026 — until live, US citizens enter with just a valid passport. EES biometric entry/exit has been live since 10 Apr 2026 (handled by border officers).

value: Third-party 'ETIAS' sites that charge extra above the €7 official fee — use only the official EU travel-europe.europa.eu portal

passport_validity_required.value: Valid for at least 3 months beyond planned departure from Schengen; 6 months recommended

onward_ticket_required.value: Generally not enforced for US tourists but technically may be requested

notes.value: Carry a photo of the passport; leave the original in the hotel safe when possible.

approx_rate_note.notes: USD/EUR is VOLATILE — serve live, never freeze

cash_vs_card.value: Cards widely accepted incl. contactless; carry €30–50 cash for small trattorias, markets, tips, and some taxis

atm_availability.value: Ubiquitous ('Bancomat'). Decline ATM 'conversion' (DCC) — always choose to be charged in EUR

tipping_norm.value: Not obligatory. Restaurants may add 'coperto' (cover charge). Rounding up or leaving €1–2/person is normal; 5–10% for excellent service. No tipping expected in bars for a coffee at the counter.

airport_train.value: Leonardo Express: non-stop FCO ↔ Roma Termini, ~30 min, €14. Cheaper FL1 regional train serves Trastevere/Ostiense/Tiburtina (~€8) but not Termini.
airport_train.notes: price stable but confirm at publish.

fl1_fare.note: Added 2026-07-13 during v2 re-platform fact retrofit; value web-verified against the official source that day; emitted to common.facts (scope rome) via rome-facts.sql. Stated ~€8 in prose; confirm exact current fare on first checker pass.

leonardo_express_fare.note: Added 2026-07-13 during v2 re-platform fact retrofit; value web-verified against the official source that day; emitted to common.facts (scope rome) via rome-facts.sql.

intercity_rail.value: Trenitalia + Italo high-speed from Termini/Tiburtina (e.g. Florence ~1h32, Naples ~1h10). Book ahead for best fares.

buses.value: ATAC city buses + trams; regional COTRAL for some day trips

domestic_flights.value: Rarely needed from Rome; rail is faster city-center to city-center for most day trips

ride_sharing.value: Uber operates only as Uber Black (premium) in Rome; FREE NOW and itTaxi apps hail licensed white taxis. Bolt also present.

car_rental_and_driving.value: Not recommended in the historic center — ZTL (limited-traffic zones) are camera-enforced with fines, parking is scarce. Drive on the RIGHT. US license + International Driving Permit required. Rent only for rural day trips, picking up outside the center.

airport_taxi_fixed_fare.value: Fiumicino (FCO) → anywhere inside the Aurelian Walls: fixed €55 (raised from €50 in 2026; confirmed by Aeroporti di Roma). Ciampino (CIA) → center: fixed €40. Flat rate covers up to 4 passengers + bags; insist on the official white taxi and the fixed fare.
airport_taxi_fixed_fare.notes: 4/5 cross-check + web sources confirm €55 for 2026; older €50 figures are out of date.

bit_fare.note: Added 2026-07-13 during v2 re-platform fact retrofit; value web-verified against the official source that day; emitted to common.facts (scope rome) via rome-facts.sql. Increase to €2.00 approved in ATAC 2025–27 plan, pending Lazio Region — watch closely.

bit_validity.note: Added 2026-07-13 during v2 re-platform fact retrofit; value web-verified against the official source that day; emitted to common.facts (scope rome) via rome-facts.sql.

transit_fine.note: Added 2026-07-13 during v2 re-platform fact retrofit; value web-verified against the official source that day; emitted to common.facts (scope rome) via rome-facts.sql.

climate_by_season.value: Hot dry summers (Jul–Aug often 30–35°C/86–95°F), mild wet winters (Dec–Feb ~8–14°C/46–57°F), warm pleasant spring & fall

value: April
May
late September
October
August (heat + many locals away, some closures)
Easter week (peak crowds at Vatican)
Christmas/New Year (busy, higher prices)

safety_health._note: YMYL. Advisory change-prone. Rome is generally very safe; main risk is petty theft.

advisory_level.value: US State Department: Level 2 'Exercise Increased Caution' for Italy (standard Europe terrorism advisory). Rome itself is very safe; main risk is petty theft.
advisory_level.notes: unanimous across cross-check; confirm current level/date at publish.

main_risk.value: Pickpocketing and bag-snatching in tourist crush (Termini, Metro line A, bus 64, Colosseum, Trevi). Violent crime against tourists is rare.

vaccines.value: None required; routine vaccinations up to date (standard EU)

tap_water_safe.value: Yes — safe and excellent. Public 'nasoni' fountains across the city give free, cold, drinkable water; bring a refillable bottle.

emergency_numbers.notes: 112 is the single universal EU number; the others still work.

value: Friendship bracelet / rose 'gifts' near Trevi & Spanish Steps
Fake petitions + pickpocket teams
Taxi overcharging / refusing the meter from Termini & FCO (fixed FCO–center fare is €50)
'Skip the line' touts reselling marked-up tickets
Bird-seed / selfie-stick hawkers
Restaurant bill padding near major sights

sim_esim.value: eSIM is easiest for US travelers (Airalo, Holafly, etc.); or local TIM/Vodafone/WindTre SIM. EU roaming included once on an EU plan.

coverage.value: Excellent 4G/5G across the city; free wifi common in cafes and many piazzas

costs._note: approximate per-person/day ranges, serve_live; USD framing for the audience

dress_norms.value: Smart-casual; Romans dress well. For churches incl. St. Peter's & the Sistine Chapel: shoulders AND knees covered (men and women) — enforced; bring a scarf/layer.

value: Do cover up for churches
Don't order cappuccino after a meal (espresso is the norm) — no one will stop you, but it marks you as a tourist
Don't sit on the Spanish Steps (fined) or eat/wade in fountains
Do validate regional train/bus tickets where required
Do greet shopkeepers with 'buongiorno/buonasera'
Don't expect dinner before ~7:30–8pm

colosseum_combined_ticket_validity.note: Added 2026-07-13 during v2 re-platform fact retrofit; value web-verified against the official source that day; emitted to common.facts (scope rome) via rome-facts.sql.

how_to_get_there.value: Walk or Metro B to Colosseo

airport.value: FCO ~45min by Leonardo Express+Metro; CIA ~40min by bus

value.name: Colosseum
value.why: book timed entry; consider arena floor

value.name: Roman Forum & Palatine
value.why: included on the combined ticket

value.name: Capitoline Museums
value.why: world's oldest public museums

value.name: The Pantheon
value.why: The world's largest unreinforced concrete dome, standing fully intact for nearly 2,000 years.

value.name: Piazza Navona
value.why: A magnificent Baroque square featuring Bernini's Fountain of the Four Rivers, built over an ancient Roman stadium.

value.name: Trevi Fountain
value.why: The iconic Baroque masterpiece where tossing a coin over your left shoulder guarantees your return to Rome.

value.who_for: First-time travelers seeking a romantic, central location steps away from major cafes and historical monuments.
Shoppers and travelers who want seamless walking access to both historic sites and premium retail districts.

value.why: avoid the tourist traps ringing the Colosseum

value.name: Armando al Pantheon
value.why: Legendary traditional Roman dining directly beside the Pantheon. Reservations are mandatory months in advance.

value.name: Frigidarium
value.why: Excellent artisanal gelato famous for its complimentary dark or white chocolate hard-shell dip.

how_to_get_there.value: Metro A to Ottaviano; walkable from Prati

airport.value: FCO ~45min by Leonardo Express+Metro; CIA ~40min by bus

value.name: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel

value.name: St. Peter's Basilica & dome
value.why: free entry, paid dome

value.name: Castel Sant'Angelo
value.why: river views, fewer crowds

value.name: Basilica of Santa Maria in Trastevere
value.why: One of Rome's oldest churches, anchoring the neighborhood with dazzling 12th-century golden mosaics.

value.name: Gianicolo Hill (Janiculum)
value.why: A short uphill hike offering panoramic, unobstructed views of the entire Roman skyline.

value.who_for: first-timers wanting calm, elegant streets near the Vatican
Travelers wanting charming, historic local character without the intense midnight noise of the central nightlife strips.

value.why: local, away from St. Peter's Square markups

value.name: Tonnarello
value.why: Incredibly popular spot serving handmade Cacio e Pepe and Carbonara out of hot skillet pans. Expect a line at peak hours.

value.name: Da Enzo al 29
value.why: A tiny, authentic Roman trattoria revered for its high-quality amatriciana, burrata, and Roman artichokes.

how_to_get_there.value: Mostly on foot; no Metro stop in the core

value.name: Castel Sant'Angelo

Families and business travelers looking for wide, safe, upscale residential grid streets featuring modern dining options.

value.why: pick streets one block off the piazzas

value.name: Pizzarium Bonci
value.why: World-famous gourmet pizza by the slice (pizza al taglio) showcasing seasonal, innovative artisanal toppings.

value.name: Hostaria Dino e Toni
value.why: An unpretentious, lively Roman trattoria known for generous portions and friendly, informal service.

how_to_get_there.value: Tram 8 from Largo Argentina, or walk across the river

value.name: Santa Maria in Trastevere

value.name: Gianicolo (Janiculum) terrace

value.name: The Colosseum
value.why: The ultimate icon of ancient Rome, once hosting spectacular gladiatorial combat and simulated sea battles.

value.name: Roman Forum & Palatine Hill
value.why: The political and commercial heart of the ancient empire, surrounded by monumental temple ruins and palaces.

value.who_for: atmosphere, nightlife, dinner — cobbled and lively
Young couples and trend-conscious travelers looking for hip vintage clothing boutiques, wine bars, and bohemian energy.

value.name_or_type: Roman trattorias (cacio e pepe, carbonara, amatriciana)
value.why: the classic dinner-and-wander quarter

value.name: La Taverna dei Fori Imperiali
value.why: A warm, family-run trattoria serving excellent local pastas and beef stews, highly popular with visiting Americans.

value.name: Regoli Pasticceria
value.why: An iconic historic pastry shop famous for serving Rome's ultimate cream-filled buns (maritozzi).

Luxury travelers seeking five-star palace hotels, designer shopping access, and high-end concierge services.

value.why: hip, walkable to the Forum

value.name: Pastificio Guerra

value.name: Mercato Testaccio

value.name: Pyramid of Cestius
value.why: An unexpected, authentic 12 BC Egyptian-style marble pyramid preserved within Rome's old city walls.

value.name: Mordi & Vai

A massive, beautifully preserved ancient Roman harbor city easily reached via a direct 30-minute local train from Piramide station.

value.name: Trattoria Vecchia Roma

Stunning Renaissance gardens featuring automated water fountains and vast, imperial Roman villa ruins.
The historic heart of the Renaissance, accessible via a fast 90-minute high-speed train ride straight from Termini.
Fast trains connect Termini to Naples in 70 minutes, allowing for seamless transfers down to the preserved archaeological site of Pompeii.

Roma Pass / Omnia Card — current prices & inclusions: SERVE LIVE, do not freeze.
